title: Logical Operators
description: Combine multiple conditions together with AND, OR, and NOT.

slides.title: Logical Operators 🧠
slides.content:
Logical operators are used to determine the logic between multiple conditions.

They let you chain multiple questions together, like 'Is the user logged in AND do they have an admin role?'

slides.title: Logical AND && 🤝
slides.content:
The `&&` (Logical AND) operator returns true if **both** statements are true.

Example: `(x > 5 && x < 10)` is true only if x is greater than 5 AND x is less than 10.

quizData.question: If x is 15, does (x > 10 && x < 20) return true?
quizData.explanation: Yes, 15 is greater than 10 AND 15 is less than 20. Both conditions are true.

slides.title: Logical OR || 🔀
slides.content:
The `||` (Logical OR) operator returns true if **one** of the statements is true.

Example: `(x == 5 || y == 5)` is true if x is 5, OR if y is 5, OR if both are 5.

quizData.question: If x is 15, what does (x == 10 || x > 10) return?
quizData.explanation: While 15 is not equal to 10 (false), it IS greater than 10 (true). Since one side is true, the OR returns true.

slides.title: Logical NOT ! 🚫
slides.content:
The `!` (Logical NOT) operator reverses the result.

If the result is true, it makes it false, and vice versa.
Example: `!(x == 5)` means 'It is NOT true that x equals 5'.

quizData.question: If bool isPlaying = true;, does !isPlaying evaluate to false?
quizData.explanation: The ! operator flips the boolean from true to false.
